reviews.review_title: Surprisingly intense and very well done
reviews.review_description: I went in expecting a decent indie horror game and came away seriously impressed. Obsessive Shadow feels polished, tense, and memorable. It knows how to make simple moments feel terrifying. If you own a Quest and enjoy horror, this is an easy recommendation.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! Glad to hear you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_description:
Another great game that I can’t fully enjoy due to lack of smooth turning option.
I find snap turning completely ruins this immersion and makes navigating the house awkward.

On the plus side the game is simple, atmospheric and has exceptional graphics for a non aaa game.

Just really needs a smooth turning option

developer_response.body:
Good news, there's a new remake as a free update coming soon with smooth turning! Stay tuned!
Join our discord server for updates:
https://discord.gg/WC6fHGwq6U

reviews.review_description: So in the beginning the shadow knocks on a window but doesnt Peter in a window to shine a light at him

developer_response.body: Hey! Apologies for the issue you're having! Just a heads up a remake is coming very soon as a free update (likely coming in next month in March)! Here's our discord if you want to get in touch with the development team! https://discord.gg/WC6fHGwq6U

reviews.review_description: This game is great with everything that it has to offer. The realism, the story, and basically everything because this game is far from amazing, it’s perfect. 10/10 would recommend to horror lovers like me 👍

developer_response.body: Thank you! So glad you enjoyed :)

reviews.review_title: Scared the crap out of me
reviews.review_description: This was a perfect moody scare. It pulled me in then boom... I almost peed my pants!

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you enjoyed a good scare! Be sure to keep your eyes out for the remake along with chapter 2!

reviews.review_description:
Indubitably exquisite virtual reality horror i am particularly pleased with this project

Drop chapter 2 brah and thanks for commenting on my video on youtube 🤞🤞🤞

developer_response.body: Thanks! Glad you enjoyed! Keep an eye out for the remake! Chapter 2 is coming 2026 :)

reviews.review_description:
Update:

As there's been no response from the developer yet, I'm going to request a refund. If it's ever updated to put the real time shadows back in I'll purchase the game again.

Original Review:

I played the demo and I was extremely impressed with the real time shadows cast by the flashlight. Few Quest games have lighting this sophisticated.  The atmosphere was excellent.

I never imagined an effect like this would be removed in an update, especially in a horror game where real time shadows greatly add to the atmosphere.

It's ironic that a game called "The Obsessive Shadow" had its shadows removed.

If the real time lighting was removed to increase resolution can an option be added to switch back to the lower resolution and keep the real time shadows? A "resolution vs graphical fidelity" switch?

The new lighting model really killed the atmosphere for me.  It's not creepy anymore. I was excited to play the full version, but after seeing it now I don't want to.

developer_response.body:
Hello, So sorry to hear you had a bad experience! I apologize for the delayed response as I have been very busy on my end. I never intended to change this and will investigate to restore the real time shadows asap. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
If you have any additional feedback, questions, concerns, or anything else please don't hesitate to join our discord server to get in touch with us:
https://discord.gg/Px6y39KkH4

reviews.review_description: Hey Pablo Im John from John’s gaming galaxy the one that has commented on a lot of your videos if anyone else is seeing this buy this game on meta buy it on steam buy it on every console when it comes out. Make Pablo rich he deserves it. It is very hard to make a VR game especially an indie game. So everyone show your love and support to Pablo. This is the best game you will ever find on VR forget about gorilla tag forget about beat saber forget about Animal Compony! This is the best game. When the second chapter comes out it will be so good. Show your support and thank Pablo for making this!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Really means a lot that you enjoy this so much :) More to come!

reviews.review_description: This game is super scary, highly recommended. I would love to see this in mixed reality.

So, I really like the idea of the game, but overall it's not technically good. 

Turning breaks this game, and not in a good way. If you're talking on the phone, or turn next to a wall, you fall in. Once this happens collision decides it's going to hide in the closet without you, as I could no longer walk up stairs, since I kept falling through the floor. 

I also noticed is there is no 3D Audio so if you are using headphones, you can't actually hear where the knock is coming from. The TV introduction sounded like it came from outside, and Mom's voice sounded like it was coming through the ceiling instead of the phone.

It's a shame I couldn't make it further then the first window scare, as this game has a lot of promise, but due to the fact that  the game breaking bugs prevent playing further, I can't recommend it.

developer_response.body: So sorry to hear you had a bad experience with the game! This feedback has been taken into account and we'll work to get this all straightened out asap! Really appreciate the feedback! Which headset did you try this on? Are you able to send videos/screenshots of your bug encounters to us through email or on our discord?

reviews.review_title: Great simple spook house game, but…
reviews.review_description:
[EDIT] ￼￼This originally was a four star rating, but I have changed it to five star due to the developers updating efforts which have enhanced the resolution quality of the game. [END EDIT]

This is the first time where I'm about to give a great review, but with a caveat to make sure people are aware of the differences between the Quest version and the one you would get from the Steam library.

I originally had this on my wishlist in Steam for quite a while. I was excited to see that it was released today. When I checked out the new launch trailer, I noticed the Meta logo at the bottom along with the other available places you can play it; Steam, and PSVR.

I figured, great, it would be stored directly on my Quest 3 headset if I just get it through the Meta store (same price). ￼

As soon as I started playing it, I could see how compressed it was. Something didn't feel right right in the very beginning with the "emergency broadcast system" words on the TV screen. ￼￼Quite a bit of aliasing as well as when things got dark you could tell there's a lot of banding when your light is hitting solid colored objects. ￼

This is not a complaint to the developer because the demo itself on Steam is 14 gigs big so clearly quite a bit of sacrificing had to be done in order for it to be a practically downloadable game onto the Quest hard drive and that's completely understandable (and expected).

When I compared the above attributes I experienced from the Quest version against the Steam demo the quality was like night and day in a much improved way with the Steam version.￼

So I definitely recommend this game overall (even on the Quest) because it will give you a very scary VR experience in general. (Especially to though who want the haunted house experience, but don't want to go through all the complicated "work" like Phasmophobia.) but I felt that it's important for everyone to be aware that even in a single house environment, such as this game, there is going to be graphical quality differences between Quest and Steam. 
￼
That being said, personally, I am going to refund the Quest version and plan to get the Steam version instead because I am fortunate enough to be able to have the options of a smooth VR experience on either platform and the Steam version is very much more polished. 

[EDIT] After getting in touch with the developer to acknowledge his efforts to update￼, I was given a game-key in order to get the game again for the Meta Quest 3 since I got it on Steam after this original review. Super nice of him to do that.

Overall, this game works really well for the standalone headset, in fact, even at its 7XX megabyte size, I feel he could un-compress it even more and get it even closer to the Steam VR resolution at a couple gigs or more big and it would still very conveniently￼ downloadable at that bigger size. [END EDIT]

Be very sure before getting this game cause if you don’t like it it’s 10 dollars you cant get back I played the game for five minutes and tried to refund the same day and it said I couldn’t cause I played past 2 hours or played past 14 days which I did not (later change since I didn’t know how to reply to dev) your game is good though I decided to try and save money for an upcoming game I’m waiting for but when I went to refund it said I was past the time and or day limit even though I did it same day and played it for a very short amount of time (2nd reply to dev) I believe Ill just give the game a chance it’s a good game so I’ll try playing longer

developer_response.body:
Very sorry to hear you had a bad experience with the game! If you’d like to tell us what went wrong we’re happy to fix it :)
We have a discord and you can reach out on pmhinteractive.com

reviews.review_title: A chilling descent into childhood fear..
reviews.review_description:
The Obsessive Shadow is more than just a horror game—it’s a nerve-wracking journey through the eyes of a terrified 9-year-old named Liam, trapped in his darkened home with something sinister lurking just beyond reach.

From the very first moments, the atmosphere grabs you. The flicker of your flashlight, the eerie silence broken by creaks and distant whispers, and the increasingly frantic voice of your mother over the phone all work together to create a creeping sense of dread. It’s not jump scares that make this game terrifying—it’s the tension, the stillness, and the fear of what might be waiting in the dark.

The storytelling is masterful. What starts as a simple blackout quickly unravels into something deeply unsettling. Is your mother really helping you? Can you trust the instructions she gives? Or are you being led deeper into danger?

The use of space and sound design is incredible, turning a familiar home into a nightmarish labyrinth. Every hallway becomes a gauntlet, and every shadow might be hiding something that sees you before you see it.

This is psychological horror done right. The Obsessive Shadow doesn’t just want to scare you—it wants to get inside your head. And it succeeds.

If you love slow-burning, atmospheric horror with rich narrative tension, The Obsessive Shadow is a must-play. Just don’t turn off your flashlight.

developer_response.body: Thank you for this extensive review! So glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: I played the Demo version of this a few years back and it scared me then, now it's somehow even more scary but in the best way. If you enjoy VR Horror games, you'll probably love this one. It's simple yet challenging, and it does it's job at scaring the living daylight out of you. The music, the voice acting, the graphics, the gameplay, everything in this game blends to create a perfectly terrifying experience. If you REALLY want to be scared, look no further, you won't be disappointed!

developer_response.body: So glad you enjoyed! Thanks for playing :)

reviews.review_description: I been waiting for the full release since it was a stranger things fan game! It's also really good! The shadow didn't look that scary in the final act but it's alright? The only complaint I have is that it's a short game

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed! Happy to hear you’re a fan! Keep an eye out for future content, this isn’t the last you’ve seen of the shadow stalker ;)

reviews.review_description: I just got the game every like 5 times he appears he isn’t in any of the windows I shine the light on all of them but I die fun game though

developer_response.body: Hello, Sorry to hear you had a bad experience with the game. I am Glad you do enjoy it though! Did you check the front door/porch window? It can appear in there, or the window above the stairs too. There's spots you really gotta look in! Thanks for the feedback, I will be taking this into account for future updates :)

reviews.review_title: One of the best options for quest
reviews.review_description: Game is literally the quest user equivalent of phasmophobia, pretty peak

developer_response.body: Thank you! Glad you enjoyed! That’s a huge compliment!

reviews.review_description: One of if not the scariest games ive ever played in my life. I felt like 13 year old me playing Silent Hill for the first time again. This has big skinamarink vibes also. Excellent work!

developer_response.body: We’re thrilled you enjoyed it! That’s a great compliment :)
